sections.paragraph:
ἐπεισέρχομαι fut. ἐπεισελεύσομαι; 2 aor. 3 pl. ἐπεισῆλθον (-θόσαν v.l. 1 Macc 16:16); pf. 3 sg. ἐπεισελήλυθεν (Just., D. 53, 2) (Hdt. et al.; pap; Philo, Op. M. 119) ‘rush in suddenly and forcibly’ (UPZ 13, 19 [160 b.c.]; 1 Macc 16:16; Jos., Ant. 11, 265) come ἐπί τινα upon someone of the Day of Judgment ἐπὶ πάντας τοὺς καθημένους ἐπὶ πρόσωπον πάσης τῆς γῆς Lk 21:35.—M-M. Lit. s. ἐπέρχομαι.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 361
